title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed with the Providence Bruins, ending his college career at Boston College after two seasons.
Hagens scored 47 points in 34 games for Boston College, helping them win their first Beanpot since 2016.
The 19-year-old was the seventh overall pick in the 2025 NHL Draft and joins Providence on an amateur tryout agreement.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer aims to improve his batting and durability as he takes on a key role in Red Sox infield.
Brayan Bello seeks consistent success to strengthen the Red Sox's already strong starting pitching rotation in 2026.
Roman Anthony, with limited MLB experience, is expected to lead Red Sox's lineup and has MVP potential.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: State Rep. Bradley Jones, Massachusetts House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection after serving 32 years in office.
Jones, 61, represents Lynnfield, Middleton, North Reading, and Reading in the 20th Middlesex District until January 2027.
He has cast over 8,180 roll call votes and served as the Republican Minority Leader since 2002.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents were deployed to Logan Airport, according to the TSA union president Mike Gayzagian.
Homeland Security implemented a "Visible Intermodal Prevention and Response" operation at Logan Airport on March 23, 2026.
ICE agents were deployed to 14 other U.S. airports due to increased wait times, but not to any New England airport.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, according to Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is acting chief following Suarez's leave, effective since February 26, 2026.
Details of the investigation remain undisclosed due to being classified as personnel matters by the city.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing outside 72 Seaport Blvd, Boston, early Sunday morning.
The victim was found with wounds to the stomach and leg and was taken to a local hospital.
No suspects have been identified and no arrests made; the investigation is ongoing, according to police.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial began in Essex County Superior Court; she's accused of assault with a dangerous weapon.
Prosecution claims Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, prompting him to fire in self-defense.
Defense argues Fitzsimmons was suicidal and Noonan misinterpreted her actions as a threat, leading to her shooting.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena following their Rhode Island state title win.
The visit included Bruins players mingling with Blackstone Valley team members, taking photos, and signing gear in the locker room.
Blackstone Valley's championship journey was marked by triumph after teammate Colin Dorgan's personal tragedy during their season.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson after his release from the New York Giants.
Hudson has played in 60 games with 19 starts, mainly at right tackle, throughout his NFL career.
Hudson provides depth for the Patriots' offensive line, potentially backing up Morgan Moses at right tackle.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens has completed his sophomore season at Boston College, but has not yet signed with the Boston Bruins.
Hagens recorded 47 points this season, and the Bruins must decide if he will start in the NHL or AHL.
Coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating young players, citing past success with college athletes adapting to pro levels.
